description: A 10-arm bandit with a hidden sequence bonus, trained via GRPO with swappable neural network policies.

problem_definition.well_posedness: Reward function R(a_t, h_t) is deterministic given action and recent history. The hidden state is progress along the secret sequence (partial-match pointer in {0..k}). Formally a POMDP bandit. Standard bandit algorithms will converge to arm 1 (0.5/round). Optimal policy earns 100/k per round amortized.

architecture_decisions.framework: PyTorch (tensors everywhere — arms, rewards, histories, Q-values, all torch.Tensor)

procedure: 1. Sample G complete trajectories from current policy
2. Score each trajectory by cumulative reward R_i = sum(r_t)
3. Compute group-relative advantages: A_i = (R_i - mean) / (std + eps)
4. All actions in trajectory i share advantage A_i
5. Update policy via clipped surrogate loss + KL penalty to reference policy
grpo_details.key_insight: No critic network. Trajectory that discovers the sequence gets massive relative advantage, reinforcing the ENTIRE action pattern.

gru.description: GRU over action history embeddings. Hidden state tracks sequence progress.
gru.recommended_for: default — right level of power for this problem

lstm.description: LSTM over action history embeddings. Separate cell state may help with long sequences.
lstm.recommended_for: longer secret sequences where GRU may forget

transformer.description: Self-attention over action history. The Transformer Bandit. Very funny.

architecture_decisions.separation_principle: core/ contains ONLY math (PyTorch tensors in, tensors out). Zero config parsing, CLI, file I/O, logging. infra/ bridges config to core via factory.py. cli/ is a thin shell over infra.

types: switching-cost bandit: cost function sigma(a_prev, a_curr) penalizes arm changes
switching policy: meta-function deciding when to swap between sub-policies

importance_sampling_replicas.description: Multiple replicas at different exploration levels. High-exploration replicas discover sequence, IS reweights experience for exploitative replicas.
importance_sampling_replicas.future_enhancement: Replica-augmented GRPO: pool all replicas' trajectories into one GRPO group.

project_structure.core/: Pure math. bandit.py, policy_network.py, grpo.py, agents.py, switching.py, replica_exchange.py, rewards.py
project_structure.config/: Pydantic schemas, JSON defaults, config loader
project_structure.infra/: Experiment runner, logger, checkpointing, factory (the ONE bridge between config and core)
project_structure.cli/: Thin CLI dispatch. Entry point: hb.py train -c <config.json>
project_structure.tests/: Core-first testing, then critical infra paths

implementation_log.action: Initial project setup and full codebase scaffolding
implementation_log.details: Created core/, config/, infra/, cli/, tests/. Implemented GRU, LSTM, Transformer policy networks. GRPO trainer. SequenceBandit environment. Pydantic configs. CLI entry point.

open_questions: Trajectory length T per episode — configurable, needs tuning relative to sequence length k
Group size G — typical range 4-64, tradeoff between variance and compute
Reference policy update cadence for GRPO KL penalty
Reward shaping / partial credit for near-sequence completions (optional)
Replica exchange with GRPO integration details
